title: US stocks today: Anthropic signs $1.8 billion AI cloud deal with Akamai
description: Artificial intelligence startup Anthropic has secured a significant computing deal worth $1.8 billion with Akamai Technologies. This agreement addresses the growing demand for Anthropic's AI software. Akamai's stock saw a substantial rise following the announcement of a long-term cloud deal with a major AI partner. The company is well-positioned to access necessary components for AI development.

title: AI Emerges As Leading Cause Of Layoffs In April As Tech Firms Cut Jobs And Shift Spending
description: New data shows artificial intelligence accounted for more than a quarter of all announced job cuts last month, while companies also cited tariffs, war-related uncertainty and cost pressures.
content:
Artificial intelligence was the leading reason companies gave for layoffs in April for the second straight month amid rising economic uncertainty and heavy spending on AI infrastructure.

title: Micron stock jumps 14% to all-time high of $742.15, skyrocketing 690% in one year: Is AI demand transforming MU stock into the heart of the semiconductor revolution?
description: Micron stock price shocked Wall Street after surged 14% and touched $738.12 in one of the biggest semiconductor rallies of 2026. The jump in MU stock is not just about earnings. It reflects a deeper AI shift inside the global economy. AI data centers now consume enormous volumes of DRAM, HBM, and NAND memory chips. That demand is reshaping the semiconductor industry itself. Micron stock has suddenly become a symbol of the AI infrastructure race powering artificial intelligence, cloud computing, and next-generation servers. Investors once viewed memory chips as cyclical commodities. Today, the market increasingly sees Micron as critical digital infrastructure for the future of AI-driven economic growth.
